pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_qgis.sh line 104 creates this file with GeoJSON content
Confirmed in task.json and export_result.sh line 20; export_result.sh creates and analyzes this file
Confirmed in task.json and export_result.sh line 21; setup_task.sh creates exports directory
Code analysis: setup_qgis.sh lines 110-111 creates exactly 2 line features with properties id 1 and 2

Verified via EPSG:IO and spatial reference databases. EPSG:32610 is WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N, which covers 126°W to 120°W. Coordinates at -122.5, 37.5 fall within this zone. Confirmed appropriate for San Francisco Bay Area (mentioned in task description and verifier.py).
Code analysis: setup_qgis.sh lines 110-111 define features with name properties exactly matching metadata

road_lengths_calculated.Road 1: 41.67 km (calculated from coordinates [-122.5, 37.5], [-122.3, 37.6], [-122.1, 37.7] using haversine formula)
road_lengths_calculated.Road 2: 33.36 km (calculated from coordinates [-122.4, 37.5], [-122.4, 37.8] using haversine formula)
road_lengths_calculated.note: These match verifier.py comment stating 'Road 1 is ~40km, Road 2 is ~33km' and fall within plausible range (0.1-500 km)

task_characteristics.input_data: 2 line features in WGS84 (EPSG:4326) covering San Francisco Bay Area
operations: Reproject from EPSG:4326 to EPSG:32610 (WGS84 to UTM10N)
Calculate road lengths using field calculator ($length / 1000)
Export as CSV with length measurements in kilometers
task_characteristics.data_is_public_or_synthetic: Synthetic data generated by setup script

privileged_info_summary: The task uses 2 synthetic road features (Road 1 and Road 2) created in setup_qgis.sh. Road 1 spans approximately 41.67 km (from coordinates [-122.5, 37.5] to [-122.1, 37.7]) and Road 2 spans approximately 33.36 km (vertical line at longitude -122.4 from latitude 37.5 to 37.8). Both roads are defined in WGS84 (EPSG:4326) covering the San Francisco Bay Area. The task requires reprojection to EPSG:32610 (WGS 84 / UTM zone 10N), which is the correct metric CRS for this geographic region. Expected outputs are a reprojected GeoJSON file (roads_utm.geojson) and a CSV file (road_measurements.csv) containing calculated road lengths.
